elements.short: Declares the existence of and describes a code system or code system supplement
elements.definition: The CodeSystem resource is used to declare the existence of and describe a code system or code system supplement and its key properties, and optionally define a part or all of its content.

elements.short: Logical id of this artifact
elements.definition: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

elements.short: Metadata about the resource
elements.definition: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

elements.short: A set of rules under which this content was created
elements.definition: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

elements.short: Language of the resource content
elements.definition: The base language in which the resource is written.

elements.short: Text summary of the resource, for human interpretation
elements.definition: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

elements.short: Contained, inline Resources
elements.definition: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, and nor can they have their own independent transaction scope.

elements.short: Additional content defined by implementations
elements.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

elements.short: Extensions that cannot be ignored
elements.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource and that modifies the understanding of the element that contains it and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

elements.short: Canonical identifier for this code system, represented as a URI (globally unique) (Coding.system)
elements.definition: An absolute URI that is used to identify this code system when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this code system is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the code system is stored on different servers. This is used in [Coding](datatypes.html#Coding).system.

elements.short: Additional identifier for the code system (business identifier)
elements.definition: A formal identifier that is used to identify this code system when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

elements.short: Business version of the code system (Coding.version)
elements.definition: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the code system when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the code system author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence. This is used in [Coding](datatypes.html#Coding).version.

elements.short: Name for this code system (computer friendly)
elements.definition: A natural language name identifying the code system. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

elements.short: Name for this code system (human friendly)
elements.definition: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the code system.

elements.short: draft | active | retired | unknown
elements.definition: The date (and optionally time) when the code system resource was created or revised.

elements.short: For testing purposes, not real usage
elements.definition: A Boolean value to indicate that this code system is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

elements.short: Date last changed
elements.definition: The date  (and optionally time) when the code system was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the code system changes.

elements.short: Name of the publisher (organization or individual)
elements.definition: The name of the organization or individual that published the code system.

elements.short: Contact details for the publisher
elements.definition: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

elements.short: Natural language description of the code system
elements.definition: A free text natural language description of the code system from a consumer's perspective.

elements.short: The context that the content is intended to support
elements.definition: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate code system instances.

elements.short: Intended jurisdiction for code system (if applicable)
elements.definition: A legal or geographic region in which the code system is intended to be used.

elements.short: Why this code system is defined
elements.definition: Explanation of why this code system is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

elements.short: Use and/or publishing restrictions
elements.definition: A copyright statement relating to the code system and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the code system.

elements.short: If code comparison is case sensitive
elements.definition: If code comparison is case sensitive when codes within this system are compared to each other.

elements.short: Canonical reference to the value set with entire code system
elements.definition: Canonical reference to the value set that contains the entire code system.

elements.short: grouped-by | is-a | part-of | classified-with
elements.definition: The meaning of the hierarchy of concepts as represented in this resource.

elements.short: If code system defines a compositional grammar
elements.definition: The code system defines a compositional (post-coordination) grammar.

elements.short: If definitions are not stable
elements.definition: This flag is used to signify that the code system does not commit to concept permanence across versions. If true, a version must be specified when referencing this code system.

elements.short: not-present | example | fragment | complete | supplement
elements.definition: The extent of the content of the code system (the concepts and codes it defines) are represented in this resource instance.

elements.short: Canonical URL of Code System this adds designations and properties to
elements.definition: The canonical URL of the code system that this code system supplement is adding designations and properties to.

elements.short: Total concepts in the code system
elements.definition: The total number of concepts defined by the code system. Where the code system has a compositional grammar, the basis of this count is defined by the system steward.

elements.short: Filter that can be used in a value set
elements.definition: A filter that can be used in a value set compose statement when selecting concepts using a filter.

elements.short: Unique id for inter-element referencing
elements.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

elements.short: Additional content defined by implementations
elements.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

elements.short: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
elements.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

elements.short: Code that identifies the filter
elements.definition: The code that identifies this filter when it is used as a filter in [ValueSet](valueset.html#).compose.include.filter.

elements.short: How or why the filter is used
elements.definition: A description of how or why the filter is used.

elements.short: = | is-a | descendent-of | is-not-a | regex | in | not-in | generalizes | exists
elements.definition: A list of operators that can be used with the filter.

elements.short: What to use for the value
elements.definition: A description of what the value for the filter should be.

elements.short: Additional information supplied about each concept
elements.definition: A property defines an additional slot through which additional information can be provided about a concept.

elements.short: Unique id for inter-element referencing
elements.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

elements.short: Additional content defined by implementations
elements.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

elements.short: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
elements.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

elements.short: Identifies the property on the concepts, and when referred to in operations
elements.definition: A code that is used to identify the property. The code is used internally (in CodeSystem.concept.property.code) and also externally, such as in property filters.

elements.short: Formal identifier for the property
elements.definition: Reference to the formal meaning of the property. One possible source of meaning is the [Concept Properties](codesystem-concept-properties.html) code system.

elements.short: Why the property is defined, and/or what it conveys
elements.definition: A description of the property- why it is defined, and how its value might be used.

elements.short: code | Coding | string | integer | boolean | dateTime | decimal
elements.definition: The type of the property value. Properties of type "code" contain a code defined by the code system (e.g. a reference to another defined concept).

elements.short: Concepts in the code system
elements.definition: Concepts that are in the code system. The concept definitions are inherently hierarchical, but the definitions must be consulted to determine what the meanings of the hierarchical relationships are.

elements.short: Unique id for inter-element referencing
elements.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

elements.short: Additional content defined by implementations
elements.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

elements.short: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
elements.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

elements.short: Code that identifies concept
elements.definition: A code - a text symbol - that uniquely identifies the concept within the code system.

elements.short: Text to display to the user
elements.definition: A human readable string that is the recommended default way to present this concept to a user.

elements.short: Formal definition
elements.definition: The formal definition of the concept. The code system resource does not make formal definitions required, because of the prevalence of legacy systems. However, they are highly recommended, as without them there is no formal meaning associated with the concept.

elements.short: Additional representations for the concept
elements.definition: Additional representations for the concept - other languages, aliases, specialized purposes, used for particular purposes, etc.

elements.short: Unique id for inter-element referencing
elements.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

elements.short: Additional content defined by implementations
elements.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

elements.short: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
elements.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

elements.short: Human language of the designation
elements.definition: The language this designation is defined for.

elements.short: Details how this designation would be used
elements.definition: A code that details how this designation would be used.

elements.short: The text value for this designation
elements.definition: The text value for this designation.

elements.short: Property value for the concept
elements.definition: A property value for this concept.

elements.short: Unique id for inter-element referencing
elements.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

elements.short: Additional content defined by implementations
elements.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

elements.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

elements.short: Reference to CodeSystem.property.code
elements.definition: A code that is a reference to CodeSystem.property.code.

elements.short: Value of the property for this concept
elements.definition: The value of this property.

elements.short: Child Concepts (is-a/contains/categorizes)
elements.definition: Defines children of a concept to produce a hierarchy of concepts. The nature of the relationships is variable (is-a/contains/categorizes) - see hierarchyMeaning.

description: The CodeSystem resource is used to declare the existence of and describe a code system or code system supplement and its key properties, and optionally define a part or all of its content.
